title: Wireshark packet list — SSDP M-SEARCH, GET /ned.exe, GET /00001292_007.jpg, MS streaming fragments
html:
Wireshark packet-list view from FINAL_forreal.pcapng showing the chronological flow: 192.168.157.134 blasting SSDP M-SEARCH broadcasts to 239.255.255.250, then HTTP GETs against Microsoft delivery edge for the filestreamingservice/files/... extension blob (the same 2026a0bb-152d-4717-... URL repeated dozens of times — Edge update telemetry), the lone GET /ned.exe, a GET /00001292_007.jpg, and Smooth-Streaming manifest + Fragments(video=...) + Fragments(Primary_1_und_384=...) for an MP4 video session.

TL;DR

  • The packet timeline confirms the lab guest was simultaneously doing SSDP discovery + Edge update polling + a Smooth-Streaming MP4 session + the /ned.exe download — all within a few minutes window.
  • Smooth-Streaming Fragments(...=N) URLs increment by ~30M ticks (3-second audio chunks) — useful detail for media-forensics scope.
  • This is the noisy-real-world traffic backdrop against which the malware analysis lab exercise asks the analyst to spot the /ned.exe C2 download.

wireshark · packet capture · network forensics · malware analysis lab · osu
